---
title: "UIAO Monthly ConMon Meeting"
subtitle: "RV5-CA07-CCM Pathway 2 — collaborative continuous monitoring"
author: "UIAO ConMon Program"
date: "2026-04-21"
categories: [conmon, fedramp, rfc-0026, ca-7, collaborative-monitoring]
---

## What this is

The **monthly UIAO ConMon meeting** is how UIAO satisfies **RV5-CA07-CCM
Pathway 2** (Collaborative Continuous Monitoring — traditional pathway)
under FedRAMP [RFC-0026](https://github.com/FedRAMP/community/discussions/130).
It is open to any agency customer of UIAO and is backed by the
machine-readable OSCAL evidence drop described below.

Operational basis: **ADR-043 D3** and **UIAO_132 §3**.

## Cadence

- **Frequency:** once per calendar month.
- **When:** published on this page and on the Connect.gov folder for UIAO
  at the start of each quarter, for all three months in that quarter.
- **Duration:** 60 minutes, with a 15-minute open-Q&A tail.
- **Format:** Microsoft Teams (GCC-Moderate tenant). Teams link
  distributed via the attendee list below.
- **Recording:** yes. Recording + transcript land in the POA&M evidence
  pack for that month.

## Standing agenda

Published **5 business days before the meeting**. Fixed items:

1. **POA&M delta since last meeting.** What opened, what closed, what
   changed severity.
2. **New critical findings.** Anything tripped into the 72-hour
   critical-finding SLA (ADR-043 D2 / UIAO_132 §2.2).
3. **Upstream change summary.** ScubaGear pin bumps, FedRAMP baseline
   / playbook revisions, NIST 800-53 updates, BOD / emergency
   directive activity.
4. **Significant Change Request review.** Any SCR filed or anticipated
   that quarter.
5. **Agency Q&A.** Open floor.

## Attendee list

Open to any agency customer of record. To be added:

- Email the UIAO ConMon program mailbox (see Connect.gov folder) with
  your agency, name, and role.
- Attendance is not gated on a specific role; observation-only is
  welcome.
- UIAO maintains the attendee list in `evidence/conmon/<yyyy-mm>/attendees.csv`
  and drops the CSV into the POA&M evidence pack for that month.

## Evidence drop

The pre-meeting evidence drop goes to Connect.gov and publishes
read-only under `evidence/oscal/<yyyy-mm>/` on the same schedule.
Three OSCAL JSON artifacts:

| Artifact | Source | Generator |
|---|---|---|
| SSP | canon + adapter registry | UIAO SSP generator (UIAO_101) |
| SAR | Drift Engine output | UIAO SAR generator (UIAO_110 driven) |
| POA&M | conformance-adapter findings | `conmon-aggregate` workflow |

Agency customers with OSCAL-capable tooling consume the same feed
UIAO's own drift ledger consumes — no bespoke reporting layer sits
between them.

## Meeting record index

A per-month record lives under `docs/docs/conmon/YYYY-MM/` with:

- `agenda.md`
- `attendees.csv` (copy of evidence-pack manifest)
- `minutes.md`
- `recording.md` (link only — binary lives in the evidence pack)
- `poa-m-delta.md` (narrative summary of the POA&M CSV delta)

The first month-record subdirectory is scheduled to land in
**2026-07** (first meeting after the RFC-0026 effective date of
2026-06-30). Until then this index is forward-looking.

## Corrective action

Missed meetings, missed evidence drops, and missed SLA triggers are
handled by the [UIAO ConMon Corrective-Action Playbook](../conmon-corrective-action-playbook.qmd) —
45-day internal cure window before the PMO strike clock fires.

## Cross-references

- **ADR-043** — FedRAMP RFC-0026 CA-7 integration decision.
- **UIAO_132** — CA-7 pathway operational specification.
- **UIAO_110** — Drift Engine (continuity between point-in-time scans).
- **FedRAMP ConMon Playbook v1.0 (2025-11-17)** — local copy under
  `src/uiao/canon/compliance/reference/fedramp-conmon-playbook/`.

## Change log

| Version | Date | Change |
|---|---|---|
| 0.1 | 2026-04-21 | Initial stub alongside ADR-043 / UIAO_132 |
